title: Prik of Conscience
summary: The General Corpus XML copies U-M's uncorrected OCR, which was created from a 372-TIFF carrier of Morris's 1863 edition. Morris's main reading text follows Cotton Galba E IX, with two precisely scoped Harley 4196 supplies, eight additional cited collation sources, and a Garrett MS 138 table of contents. The graph preserves facsimiles, access routes, and rights layers without equating Morris's unresolved H106 with the current BL catalog record.
review_notes: The 29-entity, 17-relation graph is completely classified across five primary paths and three supporting groups.
The shared digital trunk is artifact → U-M OCR encoding → 372-TIFF carrier → Morris edition; unavailable TIFF payloads do not justify bypassing the image layer.
Morris's H106 is an unidentified historical witness; the current BL Harley MS 106 record is separate conflict evidence with no lineage edge.
The complete Garrett surrogate retains the whole codex ff. 1r–130v; only the table-of-contents relation is narrowed to ff. 1r–6v / PDF pp. 4–10.
Every one of the 33 cached files has exactly one delivery owner, exact URL, size, SHA-256, coverage, and explicit target-presence decision.

entities.label: General Corpus bau1376 XML
entities.description: Pinned General Corpus copy of the U-M CME XML encoding.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME encoding BAU1376.0001.001
entities.description: Uncorrected TEI-in-Libraries Level-1 OCR electronic text created from and linked to 372 TIFF page images.

entities.label: University of Michigan 372 TIFF page-image set
entities.description: Historically linked set of 372 600-dpi TIFF G4 images of Morris's edition; the payload was not publicly retrievable during this research.

entities.label: Richard Morris, The Pricke of Conscience (1863)
entities.description: Morris's eclectic edition: Galba E IX supplies the main reading text, Harley 4196 two gaps, eight additional cited copies collation, and Garrett MS 138 the printed table of contents.

bibliographic.citation: Richard Morris, ed., The Pricke of Conscience (Stimulus Conscientiae): A Northumbrian Poem (Berlin: A. Asher & Co. for the Philological Society, 1863).
bibliographic.title: The Pricke of Conscience (Stimulus Conscientiae)
bibliographic.publisher: A. Asher & Co. for the Philological Society

entities.label: Wikimedia Commons complete scan of Morris 1863
entities.description: Complete 380-page scan of Morris's 1863 edition, content-identified against exact Commons SHA-1 and size metadata.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 11305
notes: Current record inherits obsolete Rolle attribution.

entities.label: British Library Add MS 22283
notes: Main Version component is complete inside an imperfect 172-folio composite codex.
Legacy record exposed 379 images; current full images are unavailable and Wayback preserved only one tiny tile, so no partial image was substituted for a whole-manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Galba E IX
notes: Morris's base witness.

entities.label: Unidentified manuscript cited by Morris as Harl. 106
entities.description: The manuscript Morris cited as H106. Its present identity and target extent are unresolved; it is not equated with the current BL Harley MS 106 record.
notes: This entity preserves Morris's historical source claim without asserting identity with the codex currently cataloged as British Library Harley MS 106.

entities.label: Current British Library record for Harley MS 106
entities.description: Conflict-evidence layer for the current record, which catalogs a Latin theological codex. Washington reports only Latin prose Prik-related passages at ff. 138va–vb and 344vb; no continuous Middle English target or identity with Morris's H106 is asserted.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1731
British Library Harley MS 2377
notes: East Midland recension; misbound sequence 1-33, 36-106, 34-35.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2394
notes: Imperfect at beginning; ff. 1-7 damaged; substitute leaves ff. 128-129.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4196
notes: Seven leaves missing between ff. 228 and 229; nevertheless supplies two large gaps in Galba in Morris's edition.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 6923
notes: Later sixteenth-century Rolle attribution is not authorship evidence.

entities.label: British Library Lansdowne MS 348
notes: Missing quire ff. 57-58 corresponds to lines 3842-4394; ff. 65-66 to lines 4960-5520; lines 950-1181 are displaced after line 585 and omitted in their proper place.

entities.label: Princeton University Library Garrett MS 138
entities.description: Early-fifteenth-century English codex of Prik of Conscience, possibly from Herefordshire; formerly the Yates manuscript used by Morris for his table of contents.

notes: The target work occupies the whole codex, ff. 1r–130v.
Formerly owned by Joseph Brooks Yates and Henry Yates Thompson; this identifies Morris's MS Y.
Rolle is only an attributed author in MARC; work authorship remains anonymous.

entities.label: Archived complete grayscale facsimile of Garrett MS 138
entities.description: Archived complete grayscale surrogate: 135 PDF pages containing description cards, covers, and all 130 folios.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Repository artifact through U-M OCR and page images to Morris edition
primary_transmission_paths.description: The repository artifact copies the U-M encoding; the header says that uncorrected OCR was created from the linked 372-TIFF image carrier, which reproduces Morris's edition.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Morris main reading text from Cotton Galba E IX
primary_transmission_paths.description: Morris's main reading text follows Cotton Galba E IX except for explicitly separated supplies and paratext.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Harley 4196 first supplied gap
primary_transmission_paths.description: Harley 4196 supplies the first documented gap in the Galba-based reading text.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Harley 4196 second supplied gap
primary_transmission_paths.description: Harley 4196 supplies the second documented gap in the Galba-based reading text.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Morris table of contents from Garrett MS 138
primary_transmission_paths.description: Only Morris's printed table of contents, not his main poem text, is transcribed from Garrett MS 138 ff. 1r–6v.

supporting_relationships.label: Alternative complete facsimile of the immediate edition
supporting_relationships.description: A complete independently downloaded Commons scan reproduces the same immediate edition without replacing the U-M OCR carrier in the production trunk.

supporting_relationships.label: Eight additional British Museum collation witnesses
supporting_relationships.description: Eight additional cited collation sources support Morris's apparatus. The H106 member is an unidentified historical witness, not the current BL record.

supporting_relationships.label: Complete facsimile of the table-of-contents witness
supporting_relationships.description: The complete Garrett surrogate supports inspection of the entire codex and the exact table-of-contents locus.

access.access_method: Submit the BL physical-items request form or a reference enquiry, citing the exact shelfmark and target locus.
Request the complete current codex in color, including covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every leaf, and edges if offered; separately state the target locus.
notes: Published per-image rates are not a whole-codex quote; large or difficult orders are assessed individually. Supplied images have separate permission terms.

Request fresh digitization only if the cached complete grayscale surrogate does not meet the research need.
notes: Packet rate: $2 per image for non-Princeton requesters, maximum 300 pages per project per six months, expected 6–8 weeks; verify current terms before ordering.

access.access_method: Ask the BL to reconcile Morris's historical H106 citation with the present cataloged codex before treating it as a target witness.

rights.component: repository XML artifact and markup
rights.basis: The pinned CME LICENSE is the Unlicense but expressly speaks of software; no separate XML/data/markup grant was found, so it is not silently extended to the artifact.

rights.component: current U-M encoding item statement
rights.basis: Current item says Public Domain and links RightsStatements.org No Copyright - United States; this is a rights statement, not an open license.
notes: No Copyright–United States is a rights statement, not an open license, and its practical scope must be kept separate from the older XML-header caution.

rights.component: historically linked 372 TIFF page-image set
rights.basis: The item-level No Copyright-US statement is recorded, but no separately retrievable TIFF bundle or image-specific license was verified.
notes: The item-level statement was found, but no image-specific grant or separately retrievable TIFF package was verified.

editorial_practices.description: The electronic text is uncorrected OCR linked page by page to the 372-TIFF carrier; some front matter is represented by omitted-front-matter divisions rather than full transcription.
Morris based the main reading text on Galba E IX, supplied two gaps from Harley 4196, collated eight additional cited sources, and copied the table of contents from Garrett MS 138.
editorial_practices.scope: Morris edition text, apparatus, supplied passages, and table of contents

evidence.summary: University of Michigan, 2006; uncorrected OCR; TEI in Libraries Level 1; linked to 372 600-dpi TIFF G4 page images.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan CME item BAU1376.0001.001
evidence.summary: Current item states Public Domain and links RightsStatements.org No Copyright - United States; no cache bypass was attempted after non-browser access failed.
Unlicense text speaks of software. It is not treated as a proved license for XML, data, or markup.

open_questions.question: What present physical object, if any, corresponds to the manuscript Morris cited as H106?
next_steps: Reconcile Morris's siglum through historical British Museum catalogs, accession and renumbering evidence, and reading-level comparison before adding any same-as or direct current-shelfmark edge.
Treat the current BL Harley MS 106 Latin theological codex and Washington's Latin prose loci as conflict evidence only until resolved.

open_questions.question: Does Princeton's current no-institutional-permission-required policy supersede the publication-permission instruction on the archived facsimile's embedded 1965 microfilm card?
next_steps: Ask Princeton Special Collections for a carrier-specific written interpretation before redistributing the Garrett facsimile itself.

open_questions.question: Can the historical 372-TIFF U-M page-image set be recovered with an image-layer reuse statement?
next_steps: Ask U-M for the archived page-image package and a layer-specific rights statement; do not treat the dark Internet Archive record as a file delivery.

open_questions.question: What whole-codex imaging scope, condition limits, color availability, price, and reuse permission would the BL offer for each identified witness?
next_steps: Request item-specific quotes only as needed, naming the complete codex and separate target locus.

open_questions.question: What explicit reuse license, if any, applies to the METS Prik of Conscience Introduction and wider edition?
next_steps: Seek a resource-level license; do not infer redistribution rights from the Open Access label alone.
